news.title: Spain end Messi era as Trump’s awkward stunt overshadows Yamal’s coronation
news.lede: Another FIFA World Cup has entered the history books, bringing to a close one footballing era and marking the beginning of another. Four years after Argentina’s iconic win in Qatar, Spain returned to the summit of world football in dominant fashion. In addition to securing their first World Cup title since 2010, La Furia Roja set a new European record by extending their unbeaten international run to 38 matches. On the other hand, La Albiceleste fell victim to the reigning champions’ curse in one of the most one-sided finals in recent memory.

news.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Team of the Tournament: Messi, Mbappe and Rodri make Best XI
news.lede: Spain were crowned 2026 FIFA World Cup champions after beating Argentina in Sunday’s final. Read on as we look at the Team of the tournament. Unai Simon: He won the Golden Glove award and recorded the highest save percentage. His proactive style also helped Spain maintain an effective high defensive line.

news.title: World Cup win for Spain over Argentina is a triumph for the Spanish style and their commitment to possession football
news.lede: It was not the thrilling final of four years ago, or even the carefree goal-fest of the previous evening but Spain still deserve enormous credit for dethroning Argentina in one of the more one-sided World Cup finals in memory. This was utter domination. Ferran Torres scored the winner from the twentieth shot of the match - and every single one of them came from Spain. They made 400 more passes, playing the game in Argentina's half long before a frustrated Enzo Fernandez left his team down to 10 men.

news.title: Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final
news.lede: After a 16-year wait, Spain has claimed a second men's World Cup. Ferran Torres scored the winner in a deserved 1-0 victory over Argentina, which means Spain is now men's and women's world champions. Patience proved the critical virtue for Spain. The players had to wait for a kickoff delayed by, among other things, a Tom Cruise speech.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: The tournament that changed football forever
news.lede: To view this video please enable JavaScript, and consider upgrading to a web browser that supports HTML5 video. The 2026 World Cup delivered plenty of drama, emotional farewells and packed stadiums but also brought soaring prices, political sideshows and doubts about the integrity of the competition. The 2026 FIFA World Cupwas the largest in history, featuring 48 teams, three host countries, 16 cities and 104 matches. While the expanded tournament gave more nations a chance to compete, it also brought logistical challenges, high travel costs and expensive tickets for many fans.

news.title: Argentina president announces national holiday despite team’s World Cup final loss
news.lede: Javier Milei said the date for the celebration would be up to "what the players and the coaching staff decide" after their defeat to Spain.

news.title: World Cup 2026: What are the extra time and penalty shootout rules?
news.lede: Several games in the 2026 World Cup have already ended in dramatic penalty shootouts.
